items.title: Coty Announces Agreement With Kering for Early Transition of Gucci Beauty License
items.summary: Coty agreed to hand the Gucci Beauty fragrance/cosmetics license back to Kering roughly a year early (operations continue through June 30, 2027) for about $400m total consideration ($250m at signing, $150m due by Sept 2027), resolving pending litigation between the two over the license.

items.title: Kering chute de 6,9% en Bourse a cause du ralentissement de Gucci
items.summary: Kering shares fell 6.9% to EUR247.30 (worst CAC 40 performer of the day, -17.5% YTD) after Barclays said Gucci's 2026 growth targets look increasingly unreachable; Q1 Gucci sales were down 8% and consensus expects roughly another 4.5% decline in Q2, with China and Middle East cited as continued drags ahead of July 28 half-year results.

items.title: Kering: les objectifs de Gucci "paraissent de plus en plus inatteignables" selon Barclays
items.summary: Barclays analyst note drove a 7.5% single-day drop in Kering stock, warning that Gucci's return-to-growth target looks progressively less credible given fragile consumption in key markets; Gucci represents about 40% of group revenue and 60% of operating profit, making the brand's underperformance the central swing factor for the whole group.

items.title: Moda: l'allarme dei sindacati fiorentini, il 9 luglio sciopero regionale industria Toscana
items.summary: Tuscan unions (CGIL-CISL-UIL) called a regional industry strike for July 9 in Florence over the fashion district crisis, explicitly naming Gucci, Saint Laurent, Bottega Veneta, Alexander McQueen, Balenciaga and Kering (about 6,000 Kering employees in Tuscany) among affected employers; since 2019, 830 leather-goods firms and 7,000 jobs have been lost in Florence province, with short-time work (cassa integrazione) up 250-260% in two years, and 54 threatened layoffs at Alexander McQueen a flashpoint.

items.title: Gucci Family Series 'Game Over' Reveals First-Look Images as Sky Italia Announces Further Cast
items.summary: Sky Italia released first-look images and additional cast for its six-episode Italian original 'Gucci - Fine dei Giochi', a response to Ridley Scott's 'House of Gucci' based on Allegra Gucci's memoir, starring Miriam Leone as Patrizia Reggiani and Francesco Scianna as Maurizio Gucci; the series is slated to air in 2027.

items.title: EBay 2026 Watchlist Highlights Top Luxury Resale Trends & Best Sellers
items.summary: EBay's 2026 watchlist named Louis Vuitton, Gucci, Burberry, Chanel, Prada and Dior the most-purchased resale luxury brands so far this year, with Gucci Padlock bag resale prices up 530% and Giglio bag up 428%; report also notes Mytheresa, Gucci and Jacquemus converging on Monaco for summer takeovers while Burberry activates in Athens.

items.title: Forum reaction to Gucci's Monte Carlo 2026 summer campaign
items.summary: Fashion-forum members split on Demna's Monte Carlo summer campaign (shot by Mark Seliger): some praised it as "finally looking like luxury," while others called the styling and production "tacky" and said execution was "poor" despite a promising concept referencing Gucci's Flora/Grace Kelly heritage.

items.title: Gucci Unveils the Second Chapter of Its Monte Carlo Campaign
items.summary: Descriptive coverage of chapter two of Gucci's Monte Carlo summer campaign continuing the Demna-era Flora 60th-anniversary storyline; primarily brand-supplied imagery write-up with no independent critical verdict.

items.title: Gucci official Instagram reel: Monte Carlo campaign unfolds
items.summary: Gucci's official Instagram reel promoting the Monte Carlo summer campaign; brand-owned promotional post, engagement metrics not visible/disclosed on the page as fetched.

items.title: Gucci's 2026 Reset: Luxury Giant Faces Sales Slump and AI Backlash In Major Brand Shake-up
items.summary: UK lifestyle-trade piece on Gucci's compounding troubles: weaker demand in China, softer sales in Europe/US as younger shoppers trade down, plus intensifying social-media and industry criticism of Gucci's use of AI in design/marketing, which critics say risks making products feel "less unique and more standardised."

items.title: Demna's Runway Debut at Gucci Was 'More Good Than Bad,' Says Bernstein
items.summary: Bernstein analysts scored Demna's Fall 2026 Gucci runway debut 7.6/10, calling jackets and trousers solid but shoes and handbags uninteresting and much of the rest "vulgar"; buyers and media credited Demna with a more consistent Gucci identity, though some called the show's staging an unnecessary extravagance.
